title: Method and a system for collection, categorization, validation, communication and reporting of verified data
abstract: A method and system for the real-time collection, evaluation, and communication of data relating to goods and services within a supply chain, enabling transparent compliance with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards. Through a series of interconnected modules, the system facilitates tracking, conversion, and verification of data, dynamically adjusting compliance metrics based on regional ESG regulations. Participants within the supply chain are evaluated, with their performance being represented through visual identifiers such as QR codes and incentivized through a reward system that monetizes ESG compliance. The invention promotes sustainable finance by charging variable fees based on compliance, encouraging environmentally responsible practices and supporting climate neutrality goals across industries. The evaluation of end products is based on the cumulative performance of all upstream supply chain participants, rewarding both end-users and participants for making sustainable choices.
claims:
Claims 1. A system for regulating a supply chain, the system including a platform for communicating a disclosure to an auditing authority thereby to request an evaluation in respect of a unit, for the auditing authority to assign the evaluation to the unit and linking an identifier to the evaluation and for communicating the evaluation to a participant with the supply chain. 2. A system according to claim 1 wherein the auditing authority verifies the disclosure prior to assigning the evaluation and the provision of the evaluation and linked identifier serves as confirmation of the authenticity and accuracy of the disclosure. 3. A system according to claim 1 or 2 the identifier is an instrument that can communicate comprehensive data relating to the unit. 4. A system according to claim 3 wherein the instrument is digital or can communicate the data digitally. 5. A system according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the evaluation of each participant within the supply chain is based on the evaluation of selected downstream participants. 6. A system according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the evaluation of an end-product is based on the aggregated grading or rating of each unit involved in the relevant supply chain, such that the final rating of the end-product reflects the performance and compliance of all upstream participants and units in the supply chain." 7. A system according to any one of claims 2 to 6 which includes a platform for collecting data relating to the unit, including its verified evaluation and storing such data in an immutable retrievable state. 8. A system according to claim 7 wherein a database containing the data is generated in which the data is recorded and from where it is retrieved to be communicated to a participant within a supply chain. 9. A system according to claim 8 wherein the database operates as a ledger or audit trail for any particular supply chain, recording transaction between participants, making information pertaining to each transaction available to all participants thereby informing decisions made by the participant in respect of its participation in the supply chain. 10. A system according to any one of claims 7 to 9 which provides a platform for the operation of a reward system to reward each participant based on decisions taken during its participation in the supply chain. 11. A system according to claim 10, wherein the end-user receives an evaluation based on their selection and use of end-products that have received a favorable evaluation, thereby incentivizing consumers to make sustainable and compliant purchasing choices. 12. A method for regulating a supply chain which includes the steps of; collecting and recording data relating to a unit within a supply chain, verifying the data and providing an evaluation for the unit according to its compliance in respect of industry standards and regulatory requirements, communicating such collected and recorded data between participants within the supply chain, providing a means to incentivize 

and reward participants based on compliance thereby to ensure participation in implementing the method to ensure increased visibility of all participants and associated units in the supply chain. 13. A method according to claim 12 wherein each unit is allocated an identifier which indicates it evaluation. 14. A method according to any one of claims 12 to 13 wherein the data is communicated by means of a digital platform.
